Spike-In mix preparation:
Positive control preparation:

In a LoBind 1.5mL PCR clean Eppendorf tube, prepare equal mass mix of: methylated T7 gDNA and unmethylated lambda gDNA and dilute to 20ng/µL with Qiagen EB buffer. Aliquot for single use and store at -20 °C . Prepare 1ng/µL dilution of the mix using Qiagen EB buffer, check the quant using Qubit HS Assay, aliquot for single use, and store at -20 °C for up to 3 months.

Retrieve one aliquot of 1ng/µL Spike-In mix and dilute to 6pg/µL using Qiagen EB buffer, mix very well by repeated pulse-vortexing, and aliquot single use. Store in a LoBind Eppendorf tubes at 4 °C for up to two weeks \(scPBAL Box).

In a LoBind 1.5mL PCR clean Eppendorf tube, prepare 2.5ng/µL dilution of HL60 gDNA using Qiagen EB buffer, mix very well by pipetting \(do not vortex), and check the quant using Qubit HS Assay. Aliquot for single use \(6µL/tube) and store at -20 °C for up to 6 months.

The input for this procedure is a plate containing single cells \(or nuclei) sorted into 384-well plate \(Quadrant A only – see image below for sorting plate layout) and snap frozen dry in liquid nitrogen or ethanol/dry ice bath. See figure in Figures section ( Figure. 1 ).

Retrieve an aliquot of silica beads from the 4 °C fridge \(BOX: MagSi beads). Gently pulse- vortex the tube thoroughly until the solution is uniformly dark brown and there is no longer a bead pellet on the bottom of the vial.
Prepare the MethylEdge Conversion mix using the following calculator:
Retrieve one tube of NEB 2 buffer from -20 °C and place it On ice .
Turn on the Eppendorf thermomixer and preheat to 56 °C by “spinning” at 0 rpm. Place two plate coolracks at -20 °C door shelf to chill.
Retrieve reagents needed for 1st DNA synthesis \(except enzymes) from -20 °C freezer, thaw them and place them On ice .
About 15 minutes before the program is finished start preparing for the second round of random priming.
Keep the “scPBAL DNA” plate On ice . If necessary, store the plate at -20 °C Overnight as this is a safe stopping point.
After the 30min incubation is complete, spin the plate at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute .

Spin the plate at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute . Place the plate in a pre-heated to 50 °C PCR machine \ (record PCR machine number) and close the lid. Use program: sc-lysis to incubate at 50 °C for 00:30:00 followed by a 4 °C hold \(with heated lid). While the plate is incubating, pre-treat the silica beads with DNase I \(see 'DNase I treatment of silica beads' section).
Seal the 96-well sample plate with a BioRad PCR cover using MicroAmp adhesive film applicator as follows:
To the 3 negative control wells: G2, H1, and H2 add 4 µL of Lysis buffer \(take the buffer from the Lysis buffer reservoir plate). Leave G1 well empty.

Working On ice , using P20 multichannel pipette set to 6 µL, transfer all 4 µL from each sample well into a new LoBind 96-well plate except M1, M3, O1, O3, label this plate “ scPBAL DNA ”. In the new LoBind 96-well plate, make sure to leave 4 wells empty for 3 negative controls \(G2, H1 and H2) and one positive HL60 gDNA control \(G1).
Seal the “RV” plate with a EdgeBio cover and save it for later. Seal the 384-well sample plate with EdgeBio cover and spin down at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute .

Working On ice , using a P20 multichannel pipette, add 4µL/well of Lysis buffer into each sample well of the 384-well plate \(according to plate layout – quadrant A only). After each row addition, pipette up and down 10 times to mix. Try not to create bubbles. Change tips between rows. If there is a re-arrangement of well locations - note the changes in your lab book.
Retrieve 384-well single cell sample plate from the -80 °C freezer and spin it down for 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 2 minutes . Place the plate On ice . Mark the rows and columns that contain samples.

Working On ice , aliquot 40µL/well of the Lysis brew \(4µL X 8 rows + extra) into row A of a fresh AB 1400L 96-well reservoir plate. Seal the plate with EdgeBio cover and spin down at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute . Keep the plate On ice . This plate will be used as a reservoir \(label the plate “RV” and mark the row).
After addition of all of the components, pulse-vortex the brew multiple times to assure an even distribution of all components. Quick spin the brew in a minifuge and keep it On ice .

All reagents \(except enzymes) must be pulse-vortexed and spun down in a minifuge before addition to the brew. Add enzyme last. Mix enzyme by gently flicking the tube then spin down in a minifuge before addition to the brew.
Inside the PCR work station, working On ice , in a LoBind 1.5mL tube, prepare the Lysis brew according to the following calculator: See figure in Figures section ( Figure. 2 ).

Retrieve one aliquot of 6pg/µL of Spike-In mix from the 4 °C fridge and keep it On ice . Using LoBind tube \(labeled as SC spike-in), make 100X dilution by mixing 2 µL of the Spike-In mix with 198 µL of Ultrapure distilled water \(Invitrogen). This should yield 60fg/µL concentration. Mix very well by multiple pulse-vortexing. Keep it On ice .
Retrieve one aliquot of 2.5 ng/µL stock of HL60 gDNA from 4 °C \(scPBAL Box) and keep it On ice .

Put on a new disposable lab coat, turn on the PCR work station and clean the single-cell PCR space and equipment as per instructions in General Guidelines [in Guidelines & Warnings tab]. Wipe down the ice bucket with RNase or DNA Away before placing it inside the now clean single-cell work station.

Put a fresh pair of gloves, get fresh ice and keep the ice pan outside of the PCR workstation for now. Check -20 °C freezer \(20-1) to make sure that a chilled Coolrack XT PCR96 and Coolrack M13 are ready. Turn on the PCR machine and place a piece of tape on it to indicate it is in use.
Prior to starting this procedure confirm the plate layout with the supervisor to determine the location of single cell and 10 cell wells within the 384 well plate.
After placing the PCR cover on, using adhesive film applicator apply firm pressure to press the cover onto the plate.
Run the applicator firmly from left to right several times. Run the applicator up and down several times. Create a good seal around each well.
Run the edge of the applicator around the border to seal the edge wells.
Repeat the left to right and up and down motions to assure a good final seal.

Inspect each well to make sure that each well edge is imprinted on the PCR cover. Using the applicator's edge to seal between the wells is NOT recommended. It may actually pull the seal off the wells.
Calculate total amount of beads required using the calculator below. For a full plate the volume is 230 µL . See figure in Figures section ( Figure. 3 ).

Place the plate on the Magnum FLX magnet \(wipe down the magnet before placing it inside the hood) and incubate at Room temperature for 00:02:00 until the beads separate and the solution becomes clear. See figure in Figures section ( Figure. 4 ).
Aliquot the beads equal volume \( 115 µL ) into 2 wells: B1 and B2 of the “RV” reservoir plate.
Take the plate off the magnet and resuspend the beads in each well with 115 µL of Qiagen EB buffer. Mix up and down few times.
Enzyme should be added last. Transport the enzyme from -20 °C freezer to the PCR workstation in a pre-chilled to -20 °C Coolrack M13 \(for tubes).
Add all of the non-enzymatic components first and mix very well by repeated pulse-vortexing. Keep it On ice .
All reagents \(except enzymes) must be vortexed and spun down in a minifuge before addition to the brew.
While the beads are on a magnet, working On ice , prepare DNase I master mix according to the following calculator:
Place the plate back onto the magnet for 00:02:00 until the solution becomes clear.
Using P200, carefully remove the supernatant without disturbing the beads.

After DNAse I treatment is complete, remove the plate from the PCR machine and keep it On ice . Split the mixture equally over one full row and seal the plate with EdgeBio cover. This will be 19 µL per well. DNase I treated silica beads can be stored at 4 °C Overnight .
Place the plate in a PCR machine pre-heated to 37 °C and close the lid. Use program: sc-dnase1: 37 °C for 00:30:00 , 70 °C for 00:10:00 , 4 °C hold.
Seal the plate very well with a BioRad PCR cover. Do not spin down.
Remove the plate from the magnet and resuspend each well with beads in 115 µL of DNase I master mix. Mix each well very well by pipetting up and down 10 times.
With the “RV” plate still on the magnet, carefully remove the supernatant. Do not disturb the beads.
Mix the entire brew very well by gentle pulse-vortexing, spin down in a minifuge and keep it On ice .
Immediately return the enzyme back to the -20 °C freezer before proceeding.
When pipetting the enzyme, aspirate slowly as the solution is viscous. After dispensing the enzyme into the brew, pipette up and down several times to rinse the tip.
Mix the enzyme by flicking the tube several time, quick spin in a minifuge, and place it back in the chilled tube coolrack.
After the incubation of bisulfite conversion completes, spin the plate at 3000 x g, 4 °C, 1 minute and proceed to the Desulphonation. Keep the plate covered to minimize the exposure to light.
While the plate is incubating, UV-treat the reagents as outlined in 'sc Desulphonation reagent preparation – UV treatment' section below.
Place the plate in a PCR machine, close the lid and incubate using program bs-prg1 \ (with a heated lid): 98 °C for 00:08:00 54 °C for 01:00:00 4 °C forever
Seal the plate with a BioRad PCR film and spin it down for 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute .

Using P100 single channel pipette, add 33.5 µL of MethylEdge Conversion reagent only \(without the spike-in controls) into the 2 negative control wells: H1 and H2 and mix 10 times by pipetting up and down.

Using P200 multichannel pipette, add 33.5 µL of the Conversion mix prepared above into each row of the “scPBAL DNA” plate \(except negative control wells) and mix 10 times by pipetting up and down. Change tips between rows. Make sure to manually take off the tips corresponding to the 3 negative control wells: G2, H1 and H2. Make sure to add the mix to the positive control well – G1.
Get the “scPBAL DNA” plate, add the 4 µL \( 10 ng ) HL60 gDNA into the positive control well -G1.
Aliquot the Conversion Mix 310 µL /well into one row of the “ RV ” plate. Mark the row.

Use aluminium foil to cover the inside of the ice box, shiny side up and filled with ice. Put the plates, ethanol, and NEB2 buffer in the ice box. Treat all 3 plates: “Binding + Beads”, “Desul”, “EB”, the tube of NEB 2 buffer, and both 50mL falcon tubes of 80% ethanol in a UV crosslinking machine for a total of 01:00:00 . \(UV crosslink: set to 60 minutes, located at MSL).
Using sterile 50mL falcon tubes, prepare two tubes of 50 mL 80% ethanol \(mix 40 mL of 100% ethanol with 10 mL of ultrapure water). Mix very well.

Pour Qiagen EB buffer into a clean reservoir and using P200 multichannel, aliquot 30 µL per well into a 96 well plate \(equivalent to the number of reactions). Cover the plate with a plastic seal, label “EB”, and keep it at Room temperature .
Prepare the Desulphonation plate:
Prepare Binding buffer + Beads mixture:
Get a new 96well plate and label it “Binding+Beads”.
Mix the DNase I treated silica beads in the “RV” plate by pipetting up and down at least 10X.
Using a P20 multichannel, add 2 µL of the DNase I treated beads into each well of “Binding+Beads” plate. No need to change tips between rows. Cover and save the “RV” plate.

Pour MethylEdge Binding buffer into a clean reservoir. Using P200 multichannel, aliquot 180 µL per well into “Binding+Beads” plate. Mix the beads and binding buffer very well by pipetting up and down at least 10X until it is homogenous light brown. No need to change tips between rows. Make sure that the solution is fully homogenous before moving forward. Cover with a plastic seal and keep it at Room temperature .
Collect the leftover buffer from reservoir back to Binding Buffer bottle.
Get another new 96well plate and label it “Desul”.

Pour around 8 mL MethylEdge Desulphonation buffer into a clean reservoir and using P200 multichannel, aliquot 65 µL per well into “Desul” plate \(equivalent to the number of reactions). Cover the plate with a plastic seal and keep it at Room temperature .
Bravo will then add 15 µL of QEB \(Pos6) to your DNA plate \(Pos4):
After two minutes, Bravo will prompt you with a pop-up message. Remove the cover on the QEB plate \(Pos6) before clicking ‘Continue’.
While the beads are drying, replace the plate at Pos6 with your UV-treated QEB plate.
Bravo will discard the waste fluid and perform two ethanol washes:
After the 15 minutes incubation, Bravo will prompt with a pop-up message. Follow the instructions and remove the plate cover on Pos5 before clicking ‘Continue’.
Bravo will incubate the DNA with the desulphonation buffer for 00:15:00 . Cover the DNA plate \(Pos5).
Next, Bravo will perform two 80% ethanol washes and add desulphonation buffer:
Next, Bravo will remove the binding and lysis buffer from DNA Plate \(Pos5):

Next, Bravo will prompt you to remove the reservoir from the Pos9 and replace it with a 96 well deep-well reservoir \(Waste), and to remove the DNA plate from Pos6 and replace it with the UV-treated Desulphonation buffer plate.
After incubation, Bravo will prompt you to move the ‘Beads + Binding’ plate from Pos4 to the magnet at Pos5.
Bravo will then incubate the DNA with the beads for another 00:08:00 \(total 00:10:00 ).

Bravo will retrieve tips from the tip box at Pos3 and mix the ‘Beads + Binding’ plate at Pos4 again. The tips will then be returned to Pos3. Wait until the tips are returned before covering the ‘Beads + Binding’ plate.
The program will add the DNA to the beads \(Pos6àPos4) and aliquot the ethanol \(Pos9àPos8):
Click the ‘…’ button on the right of the tip box name and change the tip state as necessary to match the tip box on the deck. Repeat for all tip boxes, and then hit ‘OK’, then ‘Continue’.
Before any tasks begin, VWorks will ask you to configure the tip state editor. Pause the protocol, and go to Tools > Tip State Editor.
If an error about a plate being detected in the gripper pops up, hit ‘Ignore’. Another error about W axis will pop up shortly thereafter. Hit ‘Retry’. The Bravo should now begin the protocol.
At the bottom of the form, hit the Start button. The Bravo will initialize.
Make sure to check if everything is positioned correctly and to remove all covers before starting of the program.

Place all needed items on the Bravo deck in their appropriate locations: Positions 1-3: new 96-well tip boxesPosition 4: coolrack+ 96 well plate \(“Binding + Beads”)Position 5: low volume magnet \(Magnum FLX)Position 6: 96 well plate containing bisul te treated DNA \(“sc-PBAL DNA”).Position 7: emptyPosition 8: new 96 well deep-well reservoir \(1 or 2mL)Position 9: Axygen HP reservoir for 80% ethanol \(Deep one)

In the VWorks form, from the ‘select protocol’ drop down menu choose: “Bisulphite Bead Clean Up”. Enter the number of rows of samples to be processed and protocol-specific parameters: Bead = 2 µL DNA = 37.5 µL Binding buffer = 180 µL Desulphonation buffer = 60 µL Elute = 15 µL
Ensure that the 96 LT head is on the Bravo. Otherwise, refer to the SOP: EGL006_Changing heads on the Agilent Bravo.
Open the PBAL VWorks form: File > Open and select: C:\VWorksWorkspace\NGS\Forms\PBAL.VWForm. To be able to see the form you must change the file type to “all types” at the bottom right of the window.
Before placing any items inside, wipe down the hood and the Bravo deck with RNAse or DNA Away.
Lift the hood sash until it is just aligned with the red arrows. Turn on the light and the fan. Turn on the switch on the right side of the Bravo and wait for a steady blue indicator light.
Turn on the Agilent computer and open VWorks software. Wait for the program to finish loading. Press the login button at the top and enter the account information.
Bravo will retrieve tips from the tip box at Pos3 and transfer the DNA from the “sc-PBAL DNA” plate at Pos6 to the ‘Binding + Beads’ plate at Pos4 and mix. The tips will then be returned to Pos3.

Bravo will retrieve tips from the tip box at Pos2 and aliquot ethanol from reservoir \(pos9) into ethanol plate \(pos8). You will need to pause the program when the tips are hovering above Pos9 to pour the fresh 80% ethanol into the reservoir. After Bravo aliquot ethanol, the tips will then be returned to Pos2. Pause the program and cover the ethanol plate at Pos8 right after Bravo drop all the tips into tip box to prevent evaporation. The evaporation in the BSC is very intense and the ethanol concentration is critical to DNA recovery. Thus, the ethanol cannot be without a cover for any extended period of time.
Before clicking ‘Continue’, place the plate on the magnet at Pos5 and incubate for 00:02:00 \(set a timer).

Click ‘Continue’ and the Bravo will incubate the plate on the magnet for 3.5 minutes set. This solution is very viscous and this additional time is necessary to allow all of the beads to settle on the magnet. This will help ensure we recover as much DNA as possible

Bravo will retrieve tips from the tip box at Pos3 and remove all of the liquid from the DNA plate \(‘Binding + Beads’ plate) at Pos5. Pause the protocol when Bravo hovers above Pos5 and uncover the plate. The tips will then be returned to Pos3. Be sure to check that all of the liquid has been aspirated as the ethanol percentage is critical in the subsequent steps.

Bravo will retrieve tips from the tip box at Pos2 and then aspirate 80% ethanol from Pos8. Pause the protocol when the Bravo machine hovers above Pos8 to remove the cover on the ethanol plate \(Pos8).
The ethanol will be gently dispensed into the DNA plate \(Pos5).
Bravo will let the ethanol solution and the beads incubate for 00:00:30 before aspirating all of the liquid from the wells \(Pos5) and dispensing the waste fluid at Pos9.
Bravo will then perform a second identical ethanol wash.

After the waste from the 2nd ethanol wash is dispensed at Pos9, pause the protocol when the Bravo hovers above the desulphonation plate \(Pos6). Remove the desulphonation plate cover \(Pos6) and cover the ethanol plate \(Pos8).
Bravo will retrieve 60 µL of desulphonation buffer from Pos6 and dispense it into the DNA plate at Pos5. The tips will then be returned to Pos2.
Bravo will retrieve tips from the tip box at Pos2 and then aspirate the used desulphonation buffer from Pos5 and discard the waste fluid at Pos9.
Bravo will then aspirate 80% ethanol from Pos8 to perform the first wash. Pause the protocol when the Bravo machine hovers above Pos8 to remove the cover on the ethanol plate \(Pos8).
The ethanol will be gently dispensed onto the DNA plate \(Pos5).
Bravo will let the ethanol solution and the beads incubate for 00:00:30 before aspirating all of the liquid from the wells \(Pos5) and dispensing the waste fluid at Pos9.
Bravo will then perform a second identical ethanol wash.
After the waste from the 2nd ethanol wash is discarded, let the DNA plate dry for 00:00:30 uncovered \(start a timer for one minute). The tips will then be returned to Pos2.
Cover the DNA plate \(Pos5) when the timer alarm goes off and let the DNA dry \(covered) for two additional minutes.
While the plate is incubating start preparation for 1st DNA synthesis.

After the beads are re-suspended in EB buffer, cover the plate with a BioRad PCR cover and place the plate in the Eppendorf thermomixer at 56 °C for 00:15:00 at 2000 rpm, °C, \(according to pop-up message). Tape the plate down to the incubator to prevent the plate from ying off.
Bravo will retrieve tips from the tip box at Pos1 and aspirate 15 µL of QEB from Pos6.
Pause the protocol when the Bravo hovers above Pos4 to dispense the QEB. Remove the cover from the DNA plate and move the plate from Pos5àPos4.
Bravo will dispense the QEB into the DNA plate at Pos4 and mix to resuspend the beads.

Set a timer for 00:15:00 and when the alarm goes off, briefly turn the thermomixer down to 1700 rpm, °C, to allow the liquid near to the top of the vortex to reincorporate into the larger volume. Then turn the thermomixer back up to 2000 rpm, °C, .
Set another timer for 00:05:00 and repeat.
Working On ice , prepare the DNA denaturation mix according to the following calculator:
Mix the pre-denaturation mix very well by repeated pulse-vortexing, spin down in a minifuge, and keep it on ice.
Aliquot 72 µL of the mix into one 12-well strip tube \( 7.25 µL **8rows+ 14 µL dead volume=72uL).
Change the tip box at position 2 on Bravo.
Pause the program and update the tip editor with Box-position2.
Place the master mix strip-tube on row A of a chilled cool-rack at position 7 on Bravo.
Aliquot 7.25 µL of pre-denaturation mix into each will of a new LoBind plate. Place this plate on a chilled cool-rack at position 6 on Bravo.

After the 56 °C incubation is complete, place the “Binding + Beads” plate in a thermocycler and close the hot lid. Keep it in for 00:00:05 to remove any condensation on the tape pad. If there is any droplet on the side of the wells, spin the plate down shortly for 00:00:05 ~ 00:00:04 .
Place the “Binding + Beads” plate back on the Bravo deck: on top of the magnet in position 5.

The robot will first aliquot the master mix brew from the 12-well strip tube into the new LoBind plate 7.25 µL/well at position 6. Then it will transfer the elute DNA 15 µL/well into the brew plate and mix.
After the program completes, seal the plate with BioRad PCR cover and spin down the “scPBAL PR” plate for 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute .
Incubate the “scPBAL PR” plate at 98 °C for 00:01:00 in a thermocycler and immediately spin down the plate at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute . Place this plate On ice , keep it COLD.
Working On ice , prepare post-denaturation mix according to the following calculator:
Vortex the buffer before addition to the brew.
Remove the enzyme from -20 °C freezer and place it in the chilled tube coolrack for transport to the PCR workstation.
Mix the enzyme by flicking the tube, spin down in a minifuge, and place it back in the chilled tube coolrack.

When you pipette the enzyme, aspirate slowly. After dispensing the enzyme into the brew, wash the tip by pipetting up and down. Place the brew back On ice . Return the enzyme back into the -20 °C freezer before proceeding.
Mix the brew very well by multiple pulse-vortexing and keep it On ice .

Aliquot 27 µL per well of the post-denaturation mix into a row of the “RV” plate. Cover and if necessary spin the plate down at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute to collect the liquid on the bottom. Place this plate in a -20 °C chilled coolracks.
Place the “scPBAL RP” plate on the other -20 °C chilled plate coolrack.

Using P20 multichannel, add 3 µL per well of the post-denaturation mix into each row of the “scPBAL RP” plate. After each row addition, set P20 multichannel to 10 µL , mix each row up and down 10 times. Change tips between rows.
After mixing of the last row, seal the plate with BioRad PCR cover, spin the plate down at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute .
Place the plate in the thermocycler, close the lid and run the program pbat-rand-a: 4 °C for 00:10:00 4 °C to 37 °C ramping at 4°C/minute 37 °C for 00:30:00 98 °C for 00:01:00 4 °C forever
Place the coolracks back in the -20 °C freezer to chill for the next step.
When ready to add the enzyme, pulse-vortex the brew multiple times, then spin down in a minifuge, and place back On ice .
All reagents \(except enzymes) must be pulse-vortexed and spin down in a minifuge before addition to the brew. Enzymes should be added last.
Working On ice , prepare 2nd DNA synthesis mix according to the following calculator:
In hood, change the PCR cover to Aluminium Cover. Seal and label with name and date, and store at -20 °C Overnight . Clean up your workstation. Remove garbage. This concludes Day 1 of this protocol.
After the program finishes, spin down the plate at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute .
Place the plate in the thermocycler, close the lid, and run the program pbat-rand-b: 4 °C for 00:00:30 4 °C to 37 °C ramping at 1 °C / 00:00:15 37 °C for 00:30:00 70 °C for 00:10:00 4 °C forever
Seal the plate with a BioRad PCR cover and spin down for 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute .

Using a P20 multichannel, add 5 µL per well of the 2nd DNA synthesis mix to each row of the “scPBAL RP” plate. After each addition, set the multichannel to 15 µL and mix up and down at least 10X, lifting your pipet when dispensing and aspirating from the bottom of the well. Change tips between rows.
Place the “scPBAL RP” plate on another -20 °C chilled coolrack.
Aliquot 45µL/well of the prepared mix into row F of “RV” plate. Cover and keep it On ice . If needed, spin down for 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute . Place this plate in a -20 °C chilled coolrack.
Once the pbat-rand program is finished, immediately spin down the “scPBAL RP” plate for 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 2 minutes . Place the plate On ice .
Mix the entire 2nd DNA synthesis mix very well by multiple pulse-vortexing, spin down in a minifuge and place it back On ice .

After dispensing the enzyme into the brew, wash the tip by pipetting up and down. After enzyme addition, place the brew immediately back On ice . Return the enzyme back into the -20 °C freezer before proceeding.
When pipetting the enzyme, aspirate slowly to assure an accurate volume transfer. Enzyme storage buffers contain glycerol, which makes the solution more viscous than water.
Mix each enzyme by flicking the tube, then spin down in a minifuge and place it back in the chilled tube cool rack.
Remove the Klenow exo – \(50U/µL) from -20 °C freezer and place it in the chilled tube cool rack for transport to the PCR workstation.
Pre-chill two coolracks at -20 °C . Put on a new disposable lab coat, clean workstation and Bravo.
Set up the Bravo exactly as stated in Bead Clean-up step 10 except with the following change in the parameters: DNA volume = 50 µLBead volume = 50 µLElution volume = 25 µLEthanol volume = 150 µL

Take out 20% PEG SeraMag beads from 4 °C \(enough for one full 96-well beads clean-up will be 6 tubes), mix by vortexing and leave them to warm at Room temperature for at least 00:30:00 . Before proceeding make sure that the SeraMag bead solution fully reached Room temperature . Using a cold solution results in reduced DNA recovery.

Retrieve the 5X Quick Ligation Buffer from -20 °C and thaw at Room temperature then transfer to ice. Mix very well before use. This reagent contains high concentration of PEG, which makes it more difficult to pipette. Take an extra care to pipette this reagent slowly to assure accuracy of volume transfer.
Aliquot 55 µL of beads into each well of the “beads” plate. Do NOT use a reservoir for this step as the beads will settle and the PEG concentration will not be consistent.
Aliquot 50 µL of beads into each well of the “beads” plate. Do NOT use a reservoir for this step as the beads will settle and the PEG concentration will not be consistent.
Work at post-PCR bench. Perform all work On ice .
Analyze qPCR data using qPCR pipeline.Rmd following instructions.
Make sure that the 20% SeraMag beads have fully reached room temperature before proceeding and use freshly made \(on the same day) 70% ethanol.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:30:00 then proceed to the next step.
After the program completes, cover the plate and spin down the plate for 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute .

The robot will first aliquot the master mix brew from the 12-well strip tube into the new LoBind plate 15 µL/well at position 6. Bravo will transfer \(Pos5) the DNA elution into the brew plate \(Pos6) and mix.
Place the master mix strip-tube on row A of a chilled cool-rack at position 7 on Bravo. Place the elution plate back on the Bravo deck: on top of the magnet in Pos5.
Change the tip box at position 2 on Bravo and pause the program and update the tip editor with Box-position2.
Working On ice , aliquot 143 µL of the mix into one 12-well strip tube.

Gently pulse-vortex the brew multiple times to assure an even distribution of all of the components. Pulse-vortexing means that you stop vortexing every time a full vortex is formed in the tube. Spin down the brew in a minifuge and immediately place it back On ice .
After enzyme addition place the brew immediately back On ice . Return the enzymes back into the -20 °C freezer before proceeding.

When pipetting the enzyme, aspirate slowly to assure an accurate volume transfer. Enzyme storage buffers contain glycerol, which makes the solution more viscous than water. After dispensing the enzyme into the brew, wash the tip by pipetting up and down. Never double dip into the solution with the same tip.

Remove the enzymes from -20 °C freezer and place them in the chilled tube cool rack for transport to the PCR workstation. Mix each enzyme by flicking the tube, then spin down in a minifuge and place it back in the chilled tube cool rack.

All reagents \(except enzymes) must be pulse-vortexed and spin down in a minifuge before addition to the brew. Enzymes should be added last. When ready to add the enzymes, pulse-vortex the end repair master mix multiple times, then spin down in a minifuge, and place back On ice .

Once SeraMag beads reach Room temperature , aliquot them into one row of a 450 µL Axygen plate.
Make sure that the DNA/reaction plate is spun down at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute before placing it on the Bravo deck.

Measure the volume of three random DNA wells \(should be 30 µL , but may be less due to evaporation) and using P20 multichannel add sufficient EB \(usually 21-24 µL) to reach 50 µL/well. After each row addition, mix well by pipetting up and down 10X. Change tips between rows. It is better to have >50 µL than to have <50 µL in this step
Retrieve “scPBAL RP” plate from -20 °C . Spin down at 3000 rpm, 4 °C, 1 minute .
Pour Qiagen EB buffer into a clean reservoir. Aliquot 100 µL of EB buffer into each well of a new AB1400 plate. Label this plate the “EB” plate.

Clean the Bravo deck with RNase or DNA Away.
Lift the hood sash until it is just above the red arrows. Turn on the light and the fan. Turn on the switch on the right side of the Agilent Bravo and wait for a steady blue indicator light.

Check the Bravo deck if all of the items are present and placed correctly. Make sure that all of the covers have been removed from plates and tip boxes before proceeding.

Aliquot SeraMag beads into one row of a 450 µL Axygen plate.

Define a cutoff for success Successful passed samples will cluster well in plot figure, the cutoff should be set right above the clustered plots. For example:
Check that there are no systematic errors with the plate

Percentage of passed samples should be above 75-80%. Check that the average CT values of the passed wells is consistent with previous sample plates \(Target and Library). Average CT value for Target should be between 21-23, average CT value for Library should be between 13-15. Delta CT between Target_Ct and Library_Ct should be between 6-9.
Working on ice, pool all of the passed wells and negative controls together into a 2mL tube. Exclude failed wells and positive controls \(usually G1 or as marked).
Measure the total volume and, working on ice, set up ethanol precipitation as follows: X \(volume pooled in µL) 0.1*X of 3M Sodium Acetate pH 5.5 \(in µL)
Mix well by pulse-vortexing. Add 1.3 µL mussel glycogen \(marked as MG, 20 mg/mL ) and mix well by pulse-vortexing.

Calculate the total volume and add 2.5X\(total volume in µL) of cold 100% ethanol. Vortex thoroughly. It is important that the solution is very well mixed. Split the volume across multiple 1.5mL tubes.
After centrifugation visually locate the pellet. Carefully discard the supernatant while avoiding the DNA pellet.
Prepare fresh 70% ethanol using ultrapure water. Take out and aliquot of 20% PEG SeraMag beads to warm up to Room temperature for at least 30 min. The following steps must be performed on ice.
Spin down at 15000 x g, 4 °C, 40 minutes \(max rpm).
After -20 °C incubation, place all tubes in the same orientation in the pre-chilled to 4 °C centrifuge.
While waiting, pre-chill the centrifuge to 4 °C .
Incubate precipitations at -20 °C for at least 2 hour but Overnight is preferred.
After centrifugation visually locate the pellet and carefully aspirate the supernatant.
Add 1 mL of 70% ethanol, plate the tube inside the centrifuge in the same orientation as before, and spin down at 15000 x g, 4 °C, 4 minutes \(max rpm).

After the pellets are fully dissolved, pool all volume into one tube. Measure the volume and top up with EB buffer to a multiple of 50 µL . For example, if the volume is 80µL add an additional 20µL of EB buffer to a total volume 100µL \(which is 2X 50µL).
When the pellet becomes clear, resuspend the DNA in each tube with 20 µL of Room temperature EB buffer. Mix up and down with the pipette.
Dry the pellet at Room temperature until the pellet becomes translucent \(maximum 5 minutes). Keep a close eye on the pellets so that they do not over-dry.
Spin down the tube in minifuge to collect all of the ethanol on the bottom. With P10 remove the final traces of ethanol.
Clean up your workstation. Remove garbage. This concludes Day 4 of this protocol.
Store the sample at -20 °C until ready for sequencing. Record all of the data in your notebook.

Use 1 µL of the sample for Qubit assay and run another 1 µL on HS Agilent to observe the fragment distribution. Expected yield should be > 1 ng/µL. Average size of the final product should be around 300bp.
After 1 min incubation, using P20 carefully transfer the supernatant \(containing the eluted DNA) into a new 1.5 mL tube. Label the tube with sample/pool ID
After 3 min elution, place the plate back onto the magnet and incubate for 00:01:00 . Make sure that all of the beads are pulled to the sides and the solution is completely clear.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:03:00 .

When the beads are dry, remove the plate from the magnet. Using P20, add 15 µL of EB buffer and mix very well by pipetting up and down. Make sure that all of the beads are re-suspended. The solution should be uniformly brown.
Let the beads dry at Room temperature for 00:02:00 . Do not over-dry the beads. You should not see any cracks.
After the second wash keep the plate still on the magnet. Using P10 aspirate the remaining traces of ethanol from the bottom of each well. Make sure not to remove any beads.
From each well, using single channel P200 set to 170µL, carefully remove all of the ethanol.
Using multichannel P200, add 150 µL 70% ethanol and incubate for 00:00:30 .
From each well, carefully aspirate the ethanol using single channel P200.
On the magnet, using multichannel P200, add 150 µL of 70% ethanol and incubate for 00:00:30 .
Without disturbing the beads, using a single channel P200, carefully remove the clear supernatant. Make sure not to remove any beads.
Keep the plate on the magnet for the next few steps.
After 10min incubation move the plate to the magnet and incubate for 00:03:00 . Make sure that all of the beads are separated to the sides and the solution is completely clear before proceeding.
Incubate at Room temperature for 00:10:00 .

Mix the 20% SeraMag beads very well and aliquot 55 µL per well into one row of a reservoir plate \ (corresponding to the number of DNA wells). Using P200 multichannel, to each 50 µL of DNA add 45 µL of Room temperature 20%PEG SeraMag beads \(0.9X ratio) and immediately mix by pipetting up and down at least 10X.
